pi_items.source: FDI (Fédération Dentaire Internationale) tooth numbering standard. Tooth 30 = quadrant 3 (lower left). Lower right first molar = quadrant 4, position 6 = tooth 46. Verified via https://pressbooks.pub/oralsurgery4rns/part/the-international-tooth-numbering-system/

FDI dental anatomy. This region is correctly described as 'lower right first molar'. However, the metadata field 'tooth_number' claims 30, which in FDI standard is in the lower LEFT quadrant, not right.
BlueSkyBio is a real dental implant manufacturer offering multiple implant systems. Verified via https://blueskybio.com/ and https://www.spotimplant.com/en/dental-implants/blue-sky-bio
BlueSkyBio manufactures 4.0mm diameter implants in their BIO | Conus 12 system with 3.5/4.0 Platform. Verified via BlueSkyBio Conus 12 product page.

BlueSkyBio BIO | Conus 12 system with 4.0mm diameter is available in lengths: 8mm, 9mm, 11mm, 13mm - but NOT 10mm. Extensive web search found no BlueSkyBio system offering 4.0 x 10mm implant. Verified via BlueSkyBio store product page at blueskybio.com/store/conus-12-implant-packs_0
Windows VM path specified in setup_task.ps1. The setup script verifies DICOM directory presence but does not access actual DICOM files which would be on the isolated VM.

Task description claims 'A real anonymized dental CBCT scan in DICOM format is available'. However, this is a Windows-based benchmark environment running on a Linux host. The actual DICOM data cannot be accessed or verified without VM access. DICOM CBCT is a standard dental format verified via https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC4598380/

privileged_info_summary: This is a dental implant treatment planning task in Blue Sky Plan 5.0 software. There is a critical metadata contradiction: the task specifies tooth_number as 30, but tooth 30 in standard FDI dental notation refers to a lower LEFT tooth, while the implant_region correctly identifies 'lower_right_first_molar' (which should be tooth 46). BlueSkyBio is confirmed as a real implant manufacturer offering 4.0mm diameter implants, but the specified implant length of 10mm cannot be verified—BlueSkyBio's 4.0mm diameter implants are available in 8, 9, 11, and 13mm lengths, not 10mm. The task runs in a Windows VM environment with DICOM data at C:\Users\Docker\Documents\DentalDICOM, but the authenticity and exact nature of this DICOM data cannot be independently verified without VM access.
